title: alectra parasitica subsp. chitrakutensis
intro: Alectra parasitica subsp. chitrakutensis, commonly known as Chitrak or Chitral, is a plant species within the Orobanchaceae family, specifically a parasitic flowering plant native to India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Alectra parasitica subsp. chitrakutensis include the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb in traditional Ayurvedic medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make decoctions, powders, or infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
